connect.title: Connect your wallet
connect.body: Vizzor binds your trial to your wallet — no email, no password. Phantom, Solflare, and any Solana Wallet Standard wallet work.

siws.title: Sign the welcome message
siws.body: We use Sign-In With Solana (SIWS) to verify it's really you. The signature stays on-device — no funds move and no transaction is sent.

trialIntro.title: Your {days}-day Pro trial is live
trialIntro.body: Get up to {dailyCap} predictions per day for the next 7 days — every signal family, every horizon. No card on file. Cancel by simply not subscribing.

error.title: Could not load alerts

modal.title: Connect a wallet
modal.footer: Solana wallets sign in via SIWS. TON wallets pay plans directly. You can switch chains anytime.

error.stale_session_hint:
{wallet} is rejecting internally — the same call that just signed worked at the wallet but the dapp got back an empty error. Try these in order:
1. {wallet} → Settings → Developer Settings → keep only ONE chain active (e.g. Solana Devnet only on localhost, mainnet only in prod).
2. {wallet} → Settings → Trusted Apps → revoke this site, then reload.
3. Lock + unlock {wallet} (clears its session cache).
4. Disable other Solana wallet extensions in this tab.
error.wrong_chain_hint:
Your {wallet} is connected to a different chain than this app needs.

1. Open {wallet} → Settings → Developer Settings.
2. Enable {chain} only.
3. Disable other testnets (e.g. Ethereum Sepolia) for this tab, then retry.

callback.signingSubtitle: Confirm the message in your wallet
callback.errorSubtitle: Try again from the page you started on

meta.title: Account · Vizzor

account.title: Account
account.sub: Everything Vizzor knows about your wallet — identity, subscription, Telegram link, and on-chain history. Nothing leaves your wallet without your signature.

subscription.title: Active plan
subscription.emptyBody: You don't have an active subscription yet. Pick a tier on /pricing — paying in SOL on Solana saves 15% instantly.
subscription.manageBody: No auto-renewal — every period is paid upfront. Cancel any time to stop the next-period nudge; your access continues to the end of the period you already paid for.
subscription.lifetimeCannotChange: Lifetime plans are non-refundable and don't expire — there's nothing to cancel or downgrade.

scheduledNotice.cancel: Cancellation scheduled — your plan continues until %date%, then drops to Free.
scheduledNotice.downgrade: Downgrade scheduled — Elite continues until %date%, then drops to Pro on your next payment.

subscription.error: Could not update — {reason}. Try again in a moment.

welcome.examples: Should I buy Bitcoin this week? | How is Solana looking the next 4h? | Show me ETH whale flows right now | What is the market saying about TON? | Sum up today in crypto | Is it worth getting into SOL today?

tray.label: Agent-payment capabilities

transfer.label: Transfer assets
transfer.tooltip: Arm to move this token between wallets. Vizzor will draft the transfer and ask you to sign before anything moves.

payment.label: Coordinate payment
payment.tooltip: Arm to schedule or condition a payment on this token. Nothing settles without a signed authorization.

tray.lockedTier: Upgrade to Pro to use agent-payments

notify.executed: ✅ Your transfer of {amount} {symbol} is on-chain — settled.
notify.executedWithTx: ✅ Your transfer of {amount} {symbol} is on-chain — settled. Tx `{tx}`.
notify.scheduled: 📅 Your payment of {amount} {symbol} is scheduled for {when}. I'll ping you here when it's ready to broadcast.
notify.paymentDue: ⏰ Your scheduled payment of {amount} {symbol} is ready. Reply or open Workflows to broadcast.
notify.rejected: You rejected the transfer of {amount} {symbol}. Nothing was sent.
notify.expired: ⌛ The transfer of {amount} {symbol} expired before it was signed. Start over to try again.

intent.risk: Vizzor will trigger the on-chain action only after you sign. Review every field — nothing here can be reversed once settled.
intent.expired: Intent expired — start over to try again.
intent.errorRejected: Signature rejected. Nothing was sent on-chain.
intent.errorNoSignPrimitive: Your wallet does not support message signing.
intent.errorWalletDisconnected: Wallet disconnected. Reconnect and try again.
intent.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Try again in a moment.

reasons.intent_expired: This intent expired before it could settle.
reasons.intent_failed: The last attempt failed on-chain. Start over.
reasons.forbidden: This intent belongs to a different wallet.
reasons.signature_invalid: Signature did not verify. Try again.
reasons.spend_cap_reached: Daily spend cap reached for this capability.
reasons.capability_capped_zero: Raise the daily cap in Settings before running this capability.
reasons.wallet_mismatch: The signing wallet did not match this session.
reasons.unsupported_network: This network is not yet supported for settlement.
reasons.upstream_timeout: The engine timed out while settling. Retry safely — no double-spend.
reasons.upstream_unreachable: The engine is unreachable. Retry when it's back.
reasons.upstream_error: The engine refused this settlement. See the details below.
reasons.engine_settlement_pending: Signature captured. On-chain settlement is queued for the engine deploy that ships with this feature — nothing has moved on-chain yet.
reasons.wallet_unexpected: Your wallet returned an unexpected error. Unlock it, close any pending prompts, and try again.
reasons.capability_tos_required: Accept the current terms to enable this capability.
reasons.tier_required: Upgrade to Pro or Elite to enable capabilities.
reasons.invalid_spend_cap: Spend cap must be a positive number up to $10 000.
reasons.invalid_tos_accepted_at: TOS acceptance timestamp is invalid — reload and try again.
reasons.unauthenticated: Sign in with your wallet to continue.
reasons.rate_limited: Too many requests. Wait a moment and retry.
reasons.capability_not_enabled: Enable this capability first, then draft the intent.
reasons.self_transfer: Recipient can't be your own wallet.
reasons.intent_transition_failed: Intent state changed mid-flight. Reload and try again.
reasons.upstream_malformed: The engine returned a shape we couldn't parse.
reasons.insufficient_balance: Your wallet doesn't hold enough SOL for this transfer plus the network fee. On devnet, top up at https://faucet.solana.com.
reasons.rpc_unavailable: The Solana RPC is unreachable right now. Try again in a moment.
reasons.wallet_preflight_failed: Your wallet's own simulation rejected the transfer. Reload the extension and retry.
reasons.capability_not_in_prod: This capability isn't shipping in this build yet. Ask us about early access.
reasons.invalid_conversation_id: Invalid conversation reference. Reload the chat and try again.
reasons.invalid_execute_at: The schedule time isn't valid. It must be at least a minute from now and at most a year out.
reasons.invalid_recurrence: That recurrence isn't supported yet — pick 'once' for now.
reasons.wallet_no_sign_message: Your wallet doesn't support message signing yet. Try Phantom or Solflare.

settings.title: Agent-payment capabilities

welcome.title: Welcome to Vizzor
welcome.body: One-minute tour. I'll point out what your wallet can do here so you know where every action lives.

composer.title: Ask anything
composer.body: Type any crypto question here — predictions, on-chain state, market color. I answer and cite my sources.

topics.title: Focus the answer
topics.body: Add a token or a skill from here to scope my reply. Great for narrowing a broad question.

carousel.title: Pick a token
carousel.body: Click a chip to activate a ticker. In-chat actions like send and pay unlock the moment a token is armed.

tray.title: Draft on-chain actions
tray.body: When a token is active, use these to draft a transfer or schedule a payment. Nothing signs without your wallet's approval.

navAlerts.title: Price alerts
navAlerts.body: Set targets here. You'll get a badge the moment one triggers — on any page you're on.

navTransactions.title: Every transaction
navTransactions.body: Every transfer or scheduled payment your wallet drafts lives here. Tap a row to see the full details.

identity.title: Your wallet, your control
identity.body: Vizzor never touches funds without a signature. Sign out any time — your history stays with your wallet.

done.title: You're ready
done.body: Ask anything, arm an alert, or draft a payment. Open Cmd+K to re-run this tour any time.

tradePlan.label: Trade plan

tradePlan.footerHint: Arming alerts fires notifications when price crosses each level.

trial.label: 7-day trial

trial.body: On your first /start, full Pro access activates automatically. After 7 days the bot auto-downgrades to Free — no card on file, no expiry surprise, no charge.

solana.name: Solana
solana.strategy: Sub-second finality. Sign once on the site and you're done.

ton.name: TON
ton.strategy: Pay from your in-Telegram wallet. Instant confirm.

trial.q: How does the 7-day Pro trial work?
trial.a: Send /start to the bot. Full Pro access is granted automatically — unlimited predictions, every tier badge, /alerts. After 7 days the bot auto-downgrades to Free. No card on file, nothing to cancel.

cancel.a: Yes. Subscriptions are per-period (monthly or annual). When the current period ends, the bot downgrades you to Free. There's no card on file to charge — you pay each period with a fresh on-chain transaction. Stop paying = automatic downgrade.

chains.q: Which chains can I pay from?
chains.a: Two native rails are live on the web: SOL on Solana (save 15%) and TON (save 10%). SOL is signed on-site; TON hands off to the Telegram bot to sign. Every payment is verified on-chain — no Stripe, no Plaid, no card data, no middleman.

difference.q: What's the practical difference between Free, Pro, and Elite?
difference.a: Free is the Scanner — sentiment, charts, top movers. Pro adds the Predictor — unlimited signals across the full horizon ladder with armed alerts. Elite adds the Whale Terminal and flow heatmap — the on-chain signal surface serious desks pay for.

refund.a: Within 48 hours of payment, message the bot with /refund and we'll send back the on-chain amount (minus network fees). After 48 hours, no refunds — but you can still cancel for the next period at any time.

connecting.label: Creating session
connecting.body: Locking the USD-to-token rate and deriving your destination address…

wrong-network.label: Wrong network
wrong-network.body: Your wallet is on a different network than the one selected. Switch networks in your wallet and try again.

signing.label: Session ready
signing.body: Connect your wallet below if you haven't already, then click Pay to sign.

paying.label: Broadcasting
paying.body: Your wallet returned the transaction. Submitting to the network and waiting for it to land…

confirming.label: Confirming
confirming.body: Payment detected. Finalizing subscription and minting grant code…

done.label: Confirmed
done.body: Payment confirmed. Redirecting to your grant code…

expired.label: Session expired
expired.body: The 5-minute rate lock elapsed. Start a new session to try again.

error.label: Error
error.body: Something went wrong. You can retry.

error.featureDisabled: Payments aren't accepted on this chain yet. Try the other Phase-1 option, or open the Telegram bot.
error.engineOffline: The Vizzor payment engine is temporarily unreachable. The site is online but can't create a session right now. Retry in a moment.
error.engineError: Payment infrastructure pending: the Vizzor engine doesn't have payment endpoints deployed yet. Use the Telegram bot to pay for now — same tier, same price.
error.engineMarkedFailed: The engine rejected this session. Retry, or contact support if it persists.
error.rateUnavailable: We couldn't lock the exchange rate. Retry in a moment.
error.rpcUnavailable: The Solana RPC is rate-limited right now. Retry in a moment, or switch to TON.
error.invalidInput: The request shape we sent the server isn't valid. Refresh the page and pick a tier from /pricing.
error.unsupportedChain: Only Solana is supported in this release. Switch to SOL on Solana.
error.priceLookup: Couldn't look up the tier price on the server. Refresh the page.
error.sessionFailed: Session creation failed. Retry, or open the Telegram bot if it persists.
error.paymentMisconfigured: Payment service is being configured. Try again shortly, or use the Telegram bot to subscribe in the meantime.
error.internalError: Payment service hit an unexpected error. Retry, or use the Telegram bot to subscribe if the issue persists.
error.insufficientBalance: Your wallet doesn't have enough SOL to cover this payment plus the network fee. Buy SOL inside your wallet's Buy flow, or send from an exchange (Coinbase, Binance, Kraken).
error.invalidDestination: The destination treasury address is misconfigured for this network. Check VIZZOR_SOLANA_TREASURY for the active cluster.
error.walletRejected: You rejected the transaction in your wallet. Click Pay again when you're ready.
error.wrongNetwork: Your wallet is on the wrong network. Switch to Solana mainnet in your wallet extension and try again.
error.unknown: Something went wrong. Retry, or use the Telegram bot if the problem persists.

pending.label: payment infrastructure pending
pending.title: Crypto checkout launches soon
pending.body: The on-site SOL checkout is built but the treasury hasn't been activated yet. In the meantime, you can pay directly through the Telegram bot — same tier, same price, same access.

prelink.cta: Skip this step next time — link your wallet to Telegram now so future payments auto-attribute.
prelink.linked: Linked to @{handle}. Future payments will skip this step.
prelink.modalBody: Open the bot and run the command below. It posts a one-time challenge you'll sign with this wallet — same key, no extra setup.

grant.label: subscription unlocked
grant.title: Redeem in Telegram
grant.body: Open the bot to activate your subscription. The grant code is embedded in the deep link — single-use, expires in 24h.
grant.qrAlt: QR code linking to the Telegram bot redemption

success.pendingBody: We're still waiting for the watcher to mint your grant code. Hold tight — usually a few seconds.
success.missingBody: The session id is missing or unknown. Start a new payment from the pricing page.

manifesto.manifestoTitle: A letter from the people building Vizzor.
manifesto.manifestoLede: This is the long version. It is the story of why Vizzor exists, what we found broken when we went looking, and what we have decided to build instead.

section1.title: §01 — Why I started this.
section1.body:
I started building Vizzor between classes on a university campus, on a laptop with a bad battery, after losing a position that I had no business taking. The trade was not the point. The point was that I had asked three different AI assistants what they thought, and all three had given me a confident, well-written, completely fabricated answer. None of them had access to real on-chain data. None of them could explain where their conviction came from. One of them quoted a price that was eleven thousand dollars off.

I sat there for a while and thought about how strange it was that the most expensive surface in finance — the one that moves twenty-four hours a day, the one with the highest velocity of capital per capita on the planet — was being served by tools that were essentially autocomplete with a price ticker glued on. The information layer was broken. Not bearish. Not in a slump. Broken.

What I wanted was simple. I wanted an instrument that read the chain directly, fused independent signals, told me how confident it was in a way I could verify, and kept a public ledger of every call it had ever made so I could see if it was actually any good. Nothing on the market did that. So I started writing it.

section2.title: §02 — What is wrong with crypto AI.
section2.body:
The dominant pattern in this space is a language model behind a chat window, fed a price feed and a system prompt that tells it to sound knowledgeable. The model produces text that has the cadence of analysis. It uses the vocabulary of analysis. It looks like analysis. None of it is grounded in anything you can audit, because there are no signals underneath, no votes, no resolved cohort, no record of what the system thought yesterday and whether yesterday's thought turned out to be true.

This is a category error, and it is a dangerous one. In markets, a fluent paraphrase that sounds correct is worse than silence. Silence makes you do your own work. A fluent paraphrase replaces your work with a guess that costs the same amount of money to act on. The user has no way to grade the system, because there is nothing to grade. There is only a sentence.

I have watched friends drain accounts following one of these systems. The system was never wrong, in the way that a horoscope is never wrong — it spoke in shapes that could be reinterpreted after the fact. That is not what I want to build. That is the opposite of what I want to build.

section3.title: §03 — The receipts are the product.
section3.body:
Every prediction Vizzor has ever emitted lives in a table called `chronovisor_predictions`. The row is written the moment the call goes out. It carries the symbol, the horizon, the direction, the calibrated confidence, the full snapshot of which signal families voted, which way they voted, what their raw and calibrated certainty factors were, and which gates the call had to clear to leave the engine. When the horizon expires, the resolved outcome is grafted on. Nothing is edited. Nothing is deleted.

That archive is public. The win-rate number on the homepage is computed from it in real time. You can filter by horizon, by tier, by chain, by session, and reproduce the number with your own eyes. If we ever publish a win rate that you cannot derive from that table, we are lying, and you should leave.

This is the part of the product that earns the rest of it. A prediction engine that hides its history is not a prediction engine. It is a marketing surface for a forecast that has never been tested. Most things in this space are the second one. We are trying to be the first.

section4.title: §04 — The value is in the synthesis, not the subscription.
section4.body:
People ask, sometimes politely and sometimes not, why Vizzor does not plug into Nansen, or Arkham, or whichever twelve-hundred-dollar-a-month intelligence stack is fashionable this quarter. The honest answer is that I do not believe those subscriptions are where the value lives. Anyone with a corporate card can rent the same feed. The interesting work is downstream of the data.

Vizzor reads whale flow, smart-money accumulation, exchange premia and stablecoin velocity directly from chain RPCs and from the free public tiers of every reputable provider. The signal families are then fused through certainty-factor algebra with weak-signal dampening, a Bayesian update with family-aware priors, and a Platt calibration that runs continuously against the resolved cohort. That pipeline is the asset. It runs on a five-dollar VPS. It does not get smarter when you bolt a twelve-hundred-dollar-a-month feed onto it.

This is a stance, not a feature list. We believe paid intelligence stacks are a tax on the assumption that more data must equal more edge. Our experience is the opposite. The edge is in what you do with the data you already have, and most teams skip that step because synthesis is harder than procurement.

section5.title: §05 — Built to outlast us.
section5.body:
The hosted Telegram bot is a convenience. It is not the product. The product is the open-source engine in the public repository, the configurable thresholds, the CLI you can pipe into your own shell scripts, the docker-compose file that brings the entire stack up on your laptop in under a minute.

We run the hosted version because most people do not want to operate infrastructure to ask a question about Bitcoin, and that is a reasonable preference. We will keep running it as long as the bill stays sane. If the bill ever stops being sane, the project does not die — every operator already has the code, the config schema, the data model, and a clear migration path off our servers. Your subscription is portable. The engine is portable. The receipts are portable.

Vizzor was built so that the people who care about it can keep using it even if we get hit by a bus, lose interest, or sell out. I think that is what software is supposed to be in 2026. I am tired of products that turn into shells the moment their company has a bad quarter, and I do not want to build one of those. So I have done what I can to make sure Vizzor is something else.

manifesto.ps: P.S. If any of this resonates, the trial is seven days, the wallet is yours, and the only cost of finding out whether the engine is real is the time it takes to ask it one honest question.

blog.blogSub: Stories, releases, and notes from the team behind Vizzor.
